extra.reasoning: The property requires mutual exclusion: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) when (prior_gi_bleed AND renal_impairment). rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) when prior_gi_bleed. These rules write to different outputs, so priority/conflict resolution (which only arbitrates among rules competing for the SAME output) cannot prevent both from firing. With prior_gi_bleed=true and renal_impairment=true, both rules fire: rec_drug_a:=true and rec_drug_b:=true. No rule sets either output to false, so the default (which applies only to outputs left unset) does not intervene. Both outputs are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion. The other inputs (qt_prolongation, elderly, ckd_dialysis) do not affect rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b and are set false for a minimal counterexample.
